title: Accept decimal number inputs in transaction and customer forms

requirements.summary: Update the TransactionEntryForm to accept decimal (floating-point) numbers for quantity, rate, and payment fields
acceptanceCriteria: User can enter decimal values (e.g., 1.5, 10.25) in quantity, rate, and payment fields
Form does not reject or truncate decimal inputs
Computed totals (total amount, net credit) correctly reflect decimal arithmetic

file_operations.description: Change input type attributes from integer-only to accept decimal values by setting step='any' or step='0.01' on the quantity, rate, and payment input fields. Update any input validation or parsing logic to use parseFloat instead of parseInt for these numeric fields.

requirements.summary: Update the AddCustomerModal to accept decimal numbers for the previous credit field
acceptanceCriteria: Previous credit field in AddCustomerModal accepts decimal values (e.g., 250.75)
Submitted value is passed as a float to the backend mutation

file_operations.description: Change the previous credit input field to accept decimal values by setting step='any' or step='0.01', and ensure the form submission logic parses the value as a float (parseFloat) before passing it to the addCustomer mutation.

requirements.summary: Update useTransactionCalculations hook to use float parsing for all numeric inputs
acceptanceCriteria: Hook returns correct decimal totals when inputs contain decimal values
No rounding or truncation of decimal values in computed results

file_operations.description: Replace all parseInt calls with parseFloat for parsing quantity, rate, previousCredit, and todayDebited inputs. Update the calculation logic to correctly compute totalAmount and netCredit using floating-point arithmetic to preserve decimal precision.
